title: I need you
wikitext:
[]: I need you             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Wed Aug 14, 2019 3:25 am to figure something out for me. Okay, I'm going to make the assumption that I need you is being said by of one of the parties on the beach non-verbally, in your mind which one is it and why? It is called One More Final: I need you, who said I need you first and when?

[]: Re: I need you             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Aug 14, 2019 6:00 am "One More Final: I Need You" is the alternate title of the entire second half of the movie , intended to complement (geddit?) the alt title of episode 26, which was "Finale: Take Care of Yourself". It does not refer to just that final scene, and it never did (though the title card being delayed until that scene is a thematically deliberate choice). It doesn't matter if/when Shinji or Asuka said the title "non-verbally" -- what does that even mean , anyway? (Are you going to ask "when did Misato non-verbally say 'Love is destructive'" next?) What matters is the theme that human beings cannot live alone and need one another despite the pain that inevitably brings.

Article: I need you
[]: I need you             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Wed Aug 14, 2019 3:25 am to figure something out for me. Okay, I'm going to make the assumption that I need you is being said by of one of the parties on the beach non-verbally, in your mind which one is it and why? It is called One More Final: I need you, who said I need you first and when?

[]: Re: I need you             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Aug 14, 2019 6:00 am "One More Final: I Need You" is the alternate title of the entire second half of the movie , intended to complement (geddit?) the alt title of episode 26, which was "Finale: Take Care of Yourself". It does not refer to just that final scene, and it never did (though the title card being delayed until that scene is a thematically deliberate choice). It doesn't matter if/when Shinji or Asuka said the title "non-verbally" -- what does that even mean , anyway? (Are you going to ask "when did Misato non-verbally say 'Love is destructive'" next?) What matters is the theme that human beings cannot live alone and need one another despite the pain that inevitably brings.
